text:
**Members vs. presence — a hard on/off switch, not independent lists.** In `build.rs:67-71`:
```rust
members: if full { member_rows(store, &channel, &input) } else { Vec::new() },
...
subchannels: if full { subchannel_rows(store, &channel) } else { Vec::new() },
```
where `full = input.cursor == 0` (`build.rs:51`). `presence_rows` is the mirror image (`people.rs:58`): `if input.cursor == 0 { return Vec::new(); }`. So:
- **Full `<members>` + `<subchannels>` render only when `cursor == 0`** — practically only on the very first turn of a session, since `cursor` is the session's `seen_cursor`, advanced to `now` right after every `turn_start` (`src/turn_context/start.rs:206-215`) and via CAS in `turn_check` (`src/daemon/server/turns.rs:135-145`).
- **`<recent-presence>` only appears on later turns**, and only for entries whose live status changed since the last cursor: `people.rs:65` `.filter(|s| s.updated_at > input.cursor)`, excluding self (`people.rs:66`).

So "why is @codex shown as a *member* with status=working" vs "why does @codex only show up in `<recent-presence>`" depends entirely on whether this is the session's first-ever fabric render or a later delta.
